candidate_summary: AMA-COUNTERBALANCE: a single fixed-direction anchor attention head per layer plus variance-triggered iato regularization that penalizes the projection of the H-1 active heads onto the anchor direction only when residual-stream variance exceeds a learned threshold.

results_analysis.title: AnchorFormer: Differentiable Anchor Attention for Efficient Vision Transformer (2025/05)
results_analysis.reason: Anchors are over tokens not heads; different artefact, no content_word substrings match, sem/func below 0.7.

results_analysis.title: Gated Attention with Asymmetric Regularization for Transformer-based Continual Graph Learning (SIGIR 2023)
results_analysis.reason: Explicit asymmetric regularization on gated attention is functionally close to AMA's asymmetric anchor-vs-free head regularizer; func >= 0.7 triggers a hit even though it is for graph attention.

results_analysis.title: Progress on Attention (Transformer Circuits 2025)
results_analysis.reason: Identifies rare/anchor heads pointing toward salient infrequent/separator tokens — head-level anchor concept that overlaps AMA's fixed-direction anchor head; sem and func both >= 0.7.

results_analysis.title: A Novel Regularization Method for Transformer Models (2025/04)
results_analysis.reason: Generic novel transformer regularization including head-variance penalty option, but no single-anchor or variance-trigger; below 0.7.

results_analysis.title: Attention Illuminates LLM Reasoning: Preplan-and-Anchor Rhythm (NeurIPS 2025)
results_analysis.reason: Anchor tokens not anchor heads; preplan-and-anchor rhythm is different artefact, below 0.7 on sem and func.

results_analysis.title: Understanding Multi-Head Latent Attention (DeepSeek MLA)
results_analysis.reason: Low-rank latent factorization without asymmetric anchor head; clearly different mechanism.

results_analysis.title: A Visual Guide to Attention Variants in Modern LLMs
results_analysis.reason: General survey of GQA/MQA/MLA with symmetric grouping; no asymmetric anchor head, below 0.7.

results_analysis.title: Linear Predictability of Attention Heads (2026/03)
results_analysis.reason: Caches full-precision reference heads and reconstructs others — asymmetric architectural treatment of heads (some serve as anchors/references, others derive from them) functionally mirrors AMA's anchor-vs-free split; both sem and func >= 0.7.

verdict_sentence: Three independent results cross the 0.7 sem/func threshold (Linear Predictability of Attention Heads, Gated Attention with Asymmetric Regularization, and Transformer Circuits rare/anchor heads) — each capturing a core component of AMA-COUNTERBALANCE (asymmetric anchor-vs-free head treatment, asymmetric attention regularization, or head-level anchor concept), so total_hits=3 >= 1 forces FAIL.
